heading: Definitions
label: 1635i Definitions

children.label: A
children.text: a person that directly or indirectly owns, controls, or holds with power to vote, 5 percent or more of the outstanding voting securities of the packer;

children.label: B
children.text: a person 5 percent or more of whose outstanding voting securities are directly or indirectly owned, controlled, or held with power to vote, by the packer; and

children.label: C
children.text: a person that directly or indirectly controls, or is controlled by or under common control with, the packer.

children.heading: Applicable reporting period
children.label: 2 Applicable reporting period
children.text: The term “applicable reporting period” means the period of time prescribed by the prior day report, the morning report, and the afternoon report, as required under .section 1635j(c) of this title

children.heading: Barrow
children.label: 3 Barrow
children.text: The term “barrow” means a neutered male swine.

children.heading: Base market hog
children.label: 4 Base market hog
children.text: The term “base market hog” means a barrow or gilt for which no discounts are subtracted from and no premiums are added to the base price.

children.heading: Boar
children.label: 5 Boar
children.text: The term “boar” means a sexually-intact male swine.

children.heading: Formula price
children.label: 6 Formula price
children.text: The term “formula price” means a price determined by a mathematical formula under which the price established for a specified market serves as the basis for the formula.

children.heading: Gilt
children.label: 7 Gilt
children.text: The term “gilt” means a young female swine that has not produced a litter.

children.label: A
children.text: barrows or gilts;

children.label: B
children.text: sows; or

children.label: C
children.text: boars or stags.

children.label: A
children.text: the formula is determined by negotiation on a lot-by-lot basis; and

children.label: B
children.text: the swine are scheduled for delivery to the packer not later than 14 days after the date on which the formula is negotiated and swine are committed to the packer.

children.heading: Noncarcass merit premium
children.label: 10 Noncarcass merit premium
children.text: The term “noncarcass merit premium” means an increase in the base price of the swine offered by an individual packer or packing plant, based on any factor other than the characteristics of the carcass, if the actual amount of the premium is known before the sale and delivery of the swine.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “other market formula purchase” means a purchase of swine by a packer in which the pricing mechanism is a formula price based on any market other than the market for swine, pork, or a pork product.

children.heading: Inclusion
children.label: B Inclusion
children.text: The term “other market formula purchase” includes a formula purchase in a case in which the price formula is based on one or more futures or options contracts.

children.label: A
children.text: is not a negotiated purchase, swine or pork market formula purchase, negotiated formula purchase, or other market formula purchase; and

children.label: B
children.text: does not involve packer-owned swine.

children.label: A
children.text: the term includes only a swine processing plant that is federally inspected;

children.label: i
children.text: a swine processing plant that slaughtered an average of at least 100,000 swine per year during the immediately preceding five calendar years; and

children.label: ii
children.text: a person that slaughtered an average of at least 200,000 sows, boars, or any combination thereof, per year during the immediately preceding five calendar years; and

children.label: C
children.text: in the case of a swine processing plant or person that did not slaughter swine during the immediately preceding 5 calendar years, the Secretary shall consider the plant capacity of the processing plant or person in determining whether the processing plant or person should be considered a packer under this part.

children.heading: Packer-owned swine
children.label: 14 Packer-owned swine
children.text: The term “packer-owned swine” means swine that a packer (including a subsidiary or affiliate of the packer) owns for at least 14 days immediately before slaughter.

children.label: A
children.text: owned by a packer (including a subsidiary or affiliate of the packer) for more than 14 days immediately before sale for slaughter; and

children.label: B
children.text: sold for slaughter to another packer.

children.heading: Pork
children.label: 16 Pork
children.text: The term “pork” means the meat of a porcine animal.

children.heading: Pork product
children.label: 17 Pork product
children.text: The term “pork product” means a product or byproduct produced or processed in whole or in part from pork.

children.label: A
children.text: hog class;

children.label: B
children.text: type of purchase; and

children.label: C
children.text: packer-owned swine.

children.label: A
children.text: hog class;

children.label: B
children.text: type of purchase; and

children.label: C
children.text: packer-owned swine.

children.heading: Sow
children.label: 20 Sow
children.text: The term “sow” means an adult female swine that has produced one or more litters.

children.heading: Swine
children.label: 21 Swine
children.text: The term “swine” means a porcine animal raised to be a feeder pig, raised for seedstock, or raised for slaughter.

children.heading: Swine or pork market formula purchase
children.label: 22 Swine or pork market formula purchase
children.text: The term “swine or pork market formula purchase” means a purchase of swine by a packer in which the pricing mechanism is a formula price based on a market for swine, pork, or a pork product, other than a future or option for swine, pork, or a pork product.

children.label: A
children.text: a negotiated purchase;

children.label: B
children.text: other market formula purchase;

children.label: C
children.text: a swine or pork market formula purchase;

children.label: D
children.text: a negotiated formula purchase; and

children.label: E
children.text: other purchase arrangement.
